name: Great-grandma's Gingerbread Cookies
recipeInstructions:
Preparation time: 30 minutes Chilling time: Overnight Baking time: 10 minutes
1. Cream the shortening and sugar in a mixing bowl, beating in the eggs one at a time. Mix the water and baking soda in a small bowl until dissolved. Add the baking soda mixture and sorghum to the butter mixture. Sift together 5-1/2 cups of flour, the spices, and salt. Blend into the dough. Divide the dough into 4 balls. Wrap in plastic wrap. Flatten and refrigerate overnight.
2. Preheat oven to 350 degrees. Roll out one portion of dough at a time on a lightly floured surface. Cut into desired shapes. Bake on a greased cookie sheet until puffed, 10-12 minutes. Do not overbake.
3. When cool, decorate with buttercream frosting and/or candies as desired. Sorghum gives these cookies a special flavor, but molasses can be used as a substitute.
